data.title: A whale sold 4,708 ETH bought two months ago, losing $2.67 million
data.description: According to Lookonchain monitoring, a whale sold 4,708 ETH ($18.8 million) bought two months ago - losing $2.67 million.

data.title: The US September CPI was slightly lower than expected, and the three major US stock indexes opened higher
data.description: At the opening bell, the Dow Jones Industrial Average rose 0.54%, the S&P 500 rose 0.7%, and the Nasdaq Composite rose 0.86%. Intel (INTC.O) opened up 6.8%, following overnight earnings reports showing a return to profitability in the third quarter, a return to revenue growth, and optimistic revenue guidance. Procter & Gamble (PG.N) rose 3.5%, with first-quarter results beating expectations. (Jinshi)

data.title: Swiss pure Bitcoin investment app Relai receives EU license under MiCA
data.description: Relai, a Swiss pure-play Bitcoin investment app, has become the latest Crypto Asset Service Provider (CASP) to be approved under the EU's crypto asset regulatory framework. Official records show that the French financial markets regulator, the Autorité des marchés financiers (AMF), issued Relai a Markets in Crypto Assets (MiCA) license on Thursday. Founded in Zurich in 2020, Relai operates a pure Bitcoin platform that offers Bitcoin buying, selling, and storage services, using a self-custodial approach that allows users full control over their assets. The MiCA license allows Relai to launch a number of regulated services related to Bitcoin investments on its app. With this license, Relai is authorized to support Single Euro Payments Area (SEPA) payments within the EU and offer higher limits on Euro transactions. (Cointelegraph)

data.title: Crypto.com files for national trust bank charter with the Office of the Comptroller of the Currency
data.description: Crypto.com announced that it has filed an application for a national trust bank charter with the U.S. Office of the Comptroller of the Currency (OCC) to advance its industry-leading custody technology and related client services, including custody and staking of assets across various blockchain and digital asset protocols, including Cronos. This filing will not impact the ongoing operations and client services of Crypto.com Custody Trust Company, a qualified custodian for non-depository trust companies regulated by the New Hampshire Department of Banking.

data.title: Aster: Plans to use 70-80% of S3 fees for ASTER repurchases, the specific allocation will depend on market conditions
data.description: Aster announced that its current goal is to allocate 70-80% of S3 fees to ASTER token buybacks. The specific allocation will depend on market conditions, and the final results will be announced after S3 concludes. The team stated that as a growing project, maintaining operational flexibility is crucial in the current uncertain market environment. Subsequent quarterly airdrops and buybacks will be announced separately once details are finalized.

data.title: The White House: It may not be possible to release inflation data next month, which would be the first time in history
data.description: The White House's Rapid Response 47 account posted that the White House has learned that next month may be the first month in history where no inflation data will be released. The Democratic-led government shutdown has prevented investigators from conducting their work in the field, preventing us from obtaining critical data. The economic impact could be severe. (Jinshi)

data.title: U.S. Inflation Rate for October Meets Expectations
data.description: According to PANews, the final expected annual inflation rate for October in the United States stands at 4.6%. This figure aligns with both the forecast and the previous value, which were also set at 4.6%. The consistency in these numbers indicates stability in inflation expectations for the period.

data.title: Cango Increases Bitcoin Holdings with Recent Mining Success
data.description: According to PANews, cryptocurrency mining company Cango announced an update revealing that it mined 138.6 bitcoins this week. This achievement brings the company's total bitcoin holdings to 6,257.9.

data.title: Goldman Sachs: CPI will not affect the Fed's interest rate cut
data.description: Lindsay Rosner, head of multi-sector fixed income investments at Goldman Sachs, said today's report wasn't enough to "spook" the Federal Reserve ahead of its expected rate cut next week, and that a December rate cut remains a possibility.

data.title: Binance Wallet's exclusive internal trading platform will adjust its fee structure on October 30th, with higher fees for the first block.
data.description: Binance Wallet's exclusive internal trading platform fee structure will be adjusted on October 30th. After a token is created, higher fees will apply to the first block, which will then decrease to the normal rate over a period of seconds. Officials said they will announce specific parameters and further details later.

data.title: Ether's Price Nears $4,000 Amid Optimistic Market Predictions
data.description:
According to Cointelegraph, Ether (ETH) demonstrated resilience as it approached the $4,000 mark on Friday, driven by optimistic market sentiment and strategic trading moves. The ETH/USD pair was observed trading at $3,940, following an intraday peak of $4,025. This price movement has sparked optimism among traders, who are anticipating further volatility and potential gains.

Trader Daab Crypto Trades highlighted the significance of surpassing the $4.1K level, which has been a long-standing cycle high. He noted that breaking above this threshold could transform the recent price dip into a substantial deviation, potentially leading to a breakout. Meanwhile, market participants are closely monitoring the upcoming Consumer Price Index (CPI) report for September, which is anticipated to reveal an inflation rate of 3.1%. Despite this, the likelihood of Federal Reserve rate cuts remains high, with odds exceeding 94% according to the CME Fedwatch tool.

Attention has also turned to an anonymous trader with a perfect win rate, who has significantly increased their long position in Ethereum to 33,270 ETH, valued at approximately $131.24 million. This trader has also initiated a 4X long position on 80 BTC, worth $8.9 million, with an entry price of $110,900 per Bitcoin. The trader's recent activities have resulted in profits exceeding $16 million, indicating a strong belief in Ethereum's potential for further price increases.

Market analysts are observing signs of accumulation among mega whales, holding between 10,000 and 100,000 ETH, which could bolster the chances of Ether breaking above the $4,000 barrier. Bullish sentiment is growing, with predictions of higher Ether prices as the $4,000 level becomes a critical point. Master of Crypto pointed out the declining supply of Ether on exchanges, suggesting that a supply squeeze could trigger a significant price surge. Fellow analyst Crypto Zee concurred, noting that Ether's price is forming a "textbook continuation" pattern, with expectations of a steady climb through the $4,250 resistance and aiming for the $4,750 demand zone.

Jelle, another market observer, maintains an optimistic outlook with a target of $10,000 for Ether, contingent on maintaining previous highs. As Cointelegraph reported, Ethereum's MVRV data and a bull flag breakout are aligning to signal renewed upward momentum, potentially driving ETH toward $4,500 by the end of October. Readers are reminded that this article does not offer investment advice, and all trading activities involve risk. It is crucial for individuals to conduct thorough research before making investment decisions.

data.title: Grayscale's Multi-Asset Crypto Fund Celebrates NYSE Listing
data.description:
According to Foresight News, Grayscale has announced the ringing of the opening bell at the New York Stock Exchange (NYSE) to celebrate the listing of its multi-asset crypto fund, the Grayscale CoinDesk Crypto 5 ETF (ticker: GDLC).

Previously known as the Grayscale Digital Large Cap Fund, GDLC is a multi-asset cryptocurrency exchange-traded product (ETP) that allows investors to invest in Bitcoin, Ethereum, Solana, XRP, and Cardano. The fund began trading on the NYSE Arca in September of this year.

data.title: Institutional analysts: This CPI data will have limited impact on the market, but may trigger discussions on December policies.
data.description: Institutional analysts comment on the US September CPI: These data will have little impact on the Fed's near-term policy discussions—next week's rate cut is a foregone conclusion. However, they will intensify discussions about what measures policymakers will take in December. If similar CPI data emerges again amidst a continued weakening job market, it will undoubtedly increase market expectations for another rate cut this year. Of course, whether government data will resume release by then remains uncertain, so many uncertainties remain. At the same time, we know that Fed policymakers are now prioritizing employment over CPI. (Jinshi)

data.title: Swiss Bitcoin App Relai Secures MiCA License from French Regulator
data.description:
According to Foresight News, Swiss Bitcoin investment app Relai has obtained a Markets in Crypto-Assets (MiCA) license from the French Financial Markets Authority (AMF). This license will allow Relai to offer several regulated Bitcoin investment services through its application.

Founded in Zurich in 2020, Relai successfully raised $12 million in a Series A funding round led by U.S. venture capital firm Ego Death Capital at the end of 2024. Other investors in the round included Plan B Bitcoin Fund, Timechain, and Solit Group.

data.title: Inflation data reinforces expectations of a rate cut, with traders betting on two more rate cuts from the Federal Reserve this year.
data.description: After the September U.S. CPI report showed consumer inflation rose slightly less than expected last month, traders increased their bets that the Federal Reserve will make two more quarter-point rate cuts this year, including one next week. Futures contracts tied to the Fed's policy rate also show rising market expectations for further rate cuts at the Fed's January meeting.

data.title: Analysts: The overall and core indicators of the US CPI in September were lower than expected, and the Federal Reserve is likely to cut interest rates next week
data.description: The US Consumer Price Index (CPI) report for September showed both headline and core figures falling short of expectations. Analysts said this suggests the Federal Reserve will almost certainly cut interest rates again next week, and the data also supports the Trump administration's view that inflation is under control and that tariffs won't trigger a surge in the cost of living. Analysts noted that the data showed gasoline prices appear to be driving the headline figures: the gasoline price index rose 4.1% in September, the largest monthly increase among all items. (Jinshi)

data.title: The "Mysterious Whale with 100% Win Rate" has a floating profit of $4.2 million
data.description: According to on-chain analyst Ai Yi's monitoring, as the CPI data came out, BTC broke through $112,000, and the floating profit of the "mysterious whale with 100% winning rate" has risen to $4.2 million.
